# Mini-Shakespearean LLM

![Loss curves](my-transformer/loss_curve.png)
<!-- Generated by plot_losses.py after training — replace with your real run's chart before submitting. -->

A from-scratch GPT-style transformer (PyTorch), trained on the tinyshakespeare
corpus in two configurations, benchmarked against each other and against a
production LLM (Gemini Flash).

*(Every member is individually responsible for understanding the full
architecture, tokenizer, and tensor shapes — see "Job Market Readiness
Clause" in the assignment.)*

## Repo layout

```
/my-transformer/     model.py, tokenizer.py, train.py, generate.py, plot_losses.py
/evaluation/          benchmark_quantitative.py, benchmark_qualitative.py
requirements.txt
README.md
```

## Setup

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Download the dataset (place at repo root as `input.txt`):

```bash
curl -o input.txt https://raw.githubusercontent.com/karpathy/char-rnn/master/data/tinyshakespeare/input.txt
```

## Model configs

| | Model A (baseline) | Model B (scaled) |
|---|---|---|
| Layers | 2 | 4 |
| Heads | 4 | 8 |
| Embedding dim | 128 | 256 |
| Context length | 64 | 128 |
| Params | ~469K | ~3.3M |

Edit `CONFIGS` in `my-transformer/train.py` if you want different numbers —
the assignment just asks for two meaningfully different scales.

## Train

```bash
cd my-transformer
python train.py --config A --steps 3000
python train.py --config B --steps 3000
python plot_losses.py    # writes loss_curve.png
```

Checkpoints land in `my-transformer/checkpoints/`, loss logs in
`my-transformer/logs/`.

## Generate

```bash
cd my-transformer
python generate.py --config A --prompt "To be, or not to " --tokens 150
python generate.py --config B --prompt "To be, or not to " --tokens 150
```

## Evaluate

Quantitative (loss + perplexity on held-out data):

```bash
cd evaluation
python benchmark_quantitative.py
```

Qualitative (3-model comparison table, requires `GEMINI_API_KEY` for the
production-model column):

```bash
export GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here
cd evaluation
python benchmark_qualitative.py
```

## Design notes for the report

- **Tokenizer**: byte-level (`vocab_size=256`), see `my-transformer/tokenizer.py`.
- **Architecture deltas from microgpt.py**: `nn.Module` + autograd instead of
  hand-rolled `Value.backward()`; vectorized `torch.matmul` instead of
  scalar loops; RMSNorm + ReLU (same choices as the blueprint).
- **Known failure modes to discuss**: repetition loops, incoherent long-range
  structure, byte-level decode artifacts on undertrained models — these are
  expected and are what the "engineering analysis of failures" grading
  criterion wants you to write about.
